name: Argument Structure: Transitive
intuition: The lexical verb encodes an event involving an external argument (agent/initiator) and an internal argument (patient/theme).
set_to_1_if: The target verb introduces an overt grammatical subject and an overt direct object noun phrase. Note that the direct object may occur as the head of a relative clause in which the target verb occurs (e.g., 'what we have done', 'the things we have seen')

The target is morphologically passive, and the lexical verb licenses both an external argument (agent) and an internal argument (patient), even if the external argument is unexpressed or realized in a 'by'-phrase. The target counts as passive only if it can be interpreted as the passive of a transitive verb with a recoverable patient.
The target verb licenses two internal arguments (ditransitive pattern), including constructions with both a direct and an indirect object.
Ambitransitive verbs are marked 1 only when an overt internal argument is present (e.g., 'He has broken the vase').
Reflexive constructions are marked 1 only when the reflexive pronoun is overt.
set_to_0_if: The target verb does not introduce an overt internal argument (direct or indirect object).
The target verb is ambitransitive and is used without an overt internal argument.
The direct object of the verb is not overt, including implied, understood, pragmatically recoverable, and semantically expected patients.
The target verb functions as a copula linking the subject to a predicative complement (e.g., 'be', 'become', 'seem'), rather than introducing an internal argument.
The target verb is a reflexive that occurs with only an implied reflexive reading and no overt reflexive pronoun.
examples_positive: She has loved the world. (Overt object -> 1)
This is what we have seen. (Direct object is head of relative clause in which verb occurs -> 1)
She has sent him the letter. (Ditransitive -> 1)
He has seen himself. (Reflexive with overt reflexive pronoun -> 1)
examples_negative: He has eaten. (Object is implied, but not overt -> 0)
The vase has broken. (Ambitransitive without external argument -> 0)
We have eaten. (Ambitrantive without overt direct object -> 0
He has shaveds. (Implied reflexive with no overt reflexive pronoun -> 0
